name: The Fool
effect: The card fizzles into a dust cloud that solidifies into a cranky old donkey, complete with saddlebags and a lead.

name: The Magician
effect: The card bursts into sparkles and settles into a beaver-fur black top hat, a pair of white gloves, a docile rabbit, and a two-headed coin.

name: The High Priestess
effect: The card swings open into the nearest solid wall, becoming a curtained doorway. It passes into whatever space was naturally on the other side of the wall.

name: The Empress
effect: The card flutters into a beautiful and elegant gown that will fit the nearest female. It is embroidered with silver thread.

name: The Emperor
effect: The card flourishes into a golden crown that fits the one who pulled the card. It is bejeweled and valued at 600 gold, but selling it openly can cause suspicion.

name: The Hierophant
effect: The card multiplies itself into the many pages of the Holy Scripture appropriate for the religion of the one who pulled the card. It is leather-bound, weighs 14 lbs. and is about the size of a breadbox.

name: The Lovers
effect: The card pulls itself into two powerful, rare earth magnets. Each has a pull of 5 lbs. and together, 9 lbs.

name: The Chariot
effect: The card dashes forth into a war horse.

name: Justice
effect: The card slams to the ground and becomes a heavy war hammer. If pulled by a cleric, the hammer has the cleric's holy symbol engraved on the side.

name: The Hermit
effect: The card flops to the earth and grows into a small stone and wooden hut with a fireplace and lockable door.

name: Wheel of Fortune
effect: The card unfolds into a sturdy market stall wagon, filled with dry goods valued at 300 gold if sold.

name: Strength
effect: The card thuds to the ground, having become a six foot long crow bar of strong iron.

name: The Hanged Man
effect: The card unwinds into a coil of bloodstained hemp rope, 50 ft. long.

name: Death
effect: The card wilts into a gleaming war scythe.

name: Temperance
effect: The card wobbles into a fine brass merchant's scale, complete with set of standard brass weights.

name: The Devil
effect: The card burns up and the ashes form a formidable trident or pitchfork (appropriate for the location).

name: The Tower
effect: The card rises from the hand into a twelve foot wooden ladder with iron hooks on one end.

name: The Star
effect: The card flashes into a lit oil lantern with a full reservoir.

name: The Moon
effect: The card rumples into a soft, black cloak of deep velour.

name: The Sun
effect: The card ignites into a lit torch that will last for four hours.

name: Judgment
effect: The card shines with a glint of light from a bright, clear hand mirror set in a rosewood frame.

name: The World
effect: The card coils into a circle that becomes a signet ring and wax set that will emboss the local kingdom's seal.
